The game itself is a very cool concept. Of course it needs work, but it is actually pretty fun. I love the music choices, the levels, and the aesthetic between levels are also amazing! Also, please ignore the negative reviews. The modding situation is being handles and if you really cant handle it, just join a private server. Tons of people are willing to join with you because they as well are also experiencing the modding issue.

So, basically, I think this game is great, however:

They need to fix Level 5, since I died basically for no reason there due to the main danger of the map (being a black thingy that runs towards you) getting stuck at the end of the map and then killing me with no option of escaping; perhaps make level 5 client-based?
When i try to join a server it doesn’t join and when i try to join public i cant join those either and if i do manage to get in a code then my hands flip backwards.
I did this with my friend and we beat it . It was really long but there were only like five levels but it was pretty good still. I wish it was more challenging and more monsters

The game feels like a one time experience more than a game. I think the ideas are unique and it has good graphics just it can’t be played more than like one or two times before becoming repetitive. Otherwise it’s fairly well made 🤤👍

I have been searching for a game with gtag physics and a frutiger aero / nostalgia esthetic and with the music that is in this game and the watch with the safety meter and showing you what you need to do just makes the game so fun so you don't get confused or lost and the monster designs are so cool I don't even think I experienced a single bug I'm excited for this game to update 😁

reviews.review_title: Good game for what it is so far
reviews.review_description:
I like a lot of the themes in this game, my favourite one would be the frutiger aero one due to its peaceful vibe and the sense (tho I wish the buildings you saw on the sky box were physical to make it feel more real rather than have it just imprinted on the wall) 

The game could step up the resolution and quality but obviously you can tell the developers are going for a retro gorilla tag type of graphics, and if I have to be honest I'm not really a fan of cause it gets repetitive. 

And also there are some bugs like how if I try to move around my arm would get stuck on the floor and the market wouldn't work when I tried to buy something.

 Speaking of repetitiveness I really hope they do something different with this game so it's not just "go through this portal, and then this dimension, then this and that" because that will get tiring, and if the developers are going to add new levels I recommend having it be saved (like a checkpoint for an example) so I don't have to start over from the beginning once I join back. 

Also the map is too big which obviously can make people a bit annoyed so maybe try to make some of the levels less time wasting or big enough to where it takes less than 10 minutes to finish it. 

And the name can be interpreted as a little bit misleading, and I'll explain why. When I saw this game on the store i thought it was gonna be a Nostalgia-based game and due to the logo I believed frutiger aero to be apart of it. Which it was, but then it transforms from that to the backrooms which had me confused.

 And also there is no options for volume, like there are some people that I try to talk to but it's literally impossible because their mics are either unintelligible or music is too loud.

But in the end I did enjoy this game a little bit and I do believe this game does have potential but it's these bugs that gives me a hard time enjoying it so I'll give it a 3 star.

This Game is so cool! It reminds me of my childhood just like what I used to do back in the day or smth like that. Gives me a good and strange feeling of nostalgia and that’s what I like because the whole game is based around it.￼

The game itself is alright but I honestly think it’s harder to edit my video with all the copyrighted music than actually do the tasks in-game. 💔

I had high expectations because I love the theme and yes the theme was good although the things you have to do in-game are extremely repetitive and there are several filler levels.

The start area also isn’t the best, the shop is filled with things from tutorials and the models don’t look original.

What I could say though is the theme and graphics look good!

Do you like dreamcore, frutiger aero, and liminal vibes? Then this game is for you! It’s really fun, not a lot of levels but there will be more in the next update! This is probably my most favorite Dreamcore game!

This game has moderators that just turn on musics very loud and there is no way to mute them when you are in the levels, also they sometimes turn on audios that say the n word many times, also I got kicked for no reason.
This game has cool levels but they are very big which is annoying but it does give feeling of the backrooms vibe.
Levels either make you run a lot or click a few buttons. I like the idea of a dreamcore type game but you could’ve at least made it enjoyable to play

Nostalgia VR is straight-up one of the most fun horror experiences I've played on Quest. The levels are pure internet nostalgia gold — Dreamcore, Weirdcore, Frutiger Aero, and multiple Backrooms vibes that hit different in VR. It's creepy in the best way, especially when you're playing with friends. The multiplayer makes it even better because you get to freak out together.

The atmosphere is spot on. It perfectly captures that liminal, "this feels familiar but wrong" feeling that makes these aesthetics so addictive. Performance is smooth, the environments look great, and there's a ton of replay value exploring all the different levels.
If you love nostalgic liminal spaces, Backrooms, or just want a fun horror game to play with the squad, download this right now. The devs nailed it. Easily one of my favorite recent Quest games.
Highly recommend! Can't wait to see what they add next.


(Posted by a content creator who makes VR videos — this one earned a full playthrough on my channel!)
